Magnaporthe oryzae (Fungus)
Rice Blast
Diamond-shaped lesions on leaves with gray centers and brown margins. Neck rot causing panicles to fall over.
Fungal infection favored by high humidity and nitrogen fertilization.
Ganoderma boninense (Fungus)
Basal Stem Rot (BSR)
Unopened spears (fronds), yellowing of lower fronds, decay at the base of the trunk.
Soil-borne fungus that attacks the oil palm root system and trunk base.
Rigidoporus microporus (Fungus)
White Root Disease
Foliage turns yellowish, white fungal rhizomorphs on root surface, eventually tree death.
Spread through root contact with infected debris or neighboring trees.
Metisa plana / Mahasena corbetti (Insect)
Bagworms
Defoliation of fronds, scorched appearance of leaves, presence of small bags/cases hanging from leaves.
Larval feeding of psychid moths, often triggered by imbalance in natural predators.
Oidium heveae / Podisphaera (Fungus)
White powdery coating on leaves, stems, and fruits. Leaves may yellow and drop.
Fungal infection favored by high humidity and moderate temperatures.
Colletotrichum species (Fungus)
Anthracnose
Dark, sunken lesions on fruits. Brown spots on leaves. Premature fruit drop.
Common in mangoes, papayas, bananas during wet seasons.
Phytophthora palmivora (Oomycete/Water Mold)
Stem Canker
Dark, water-soaked patches on the bark, reddish-brown resin exudation, bark cracking.
Soil-borne pathogen favored by high rainfall and poor drainage in Durian orchards.
Conogethes punctiferalis / Mudaria (Insect)
Fruit Borer
Holes on fruit surface with frass (insect droppings), rotting of internal fruit pulp.
Larvae boring into fruits to feed on seeds and pulp.
Erwinia chrysanthemi (Bacteria)
Bacterial Heart Rot
Water-soaked lesions on central leaves, foul-smelling rot, primary leaves easily pulled out.
Bacterial infection through wounds or natural openings in wet conditions.
Fusarium Wilt
Yellowing of lower leaves progressing upward. Wilting during day. Brown vascular discoloration.
Soil-borne fungus (Fusarium oxysporum) blocking water transport.
Downy Mildew
Yellow patches on upper leaf surface. Gray-purple fuzzy growth on undersides.
Fungal-like organism (Peronospora) thriving in cool, wet conditions.
Black Spot Disease
Circular black spots with yellow halos on leaves. Premature leaf drop.
Fungal infection spread by water splash.
Rust Disease
Orange, yellow, or brown pustules on leaf undersides. Premature leaf drop.
Fungal spores (Puccinia) spread by wind.
Aphid Infestation
Clusters of small soft-bodied insects on new growth. Curled, distorted leaves. Sticky honeydew on leaves.
Aphids (various species) sucking plant sap. Spread plant viruses.
Whitefly Damage
Tiny white flying insects on leaf undersides. Yellowing leaves. Sooty mold growth.
Whiteflies feeding on plant sap and transmitting viruses.
Fruit Fly Damage
Small puncture marks on fruit skin. Soft, rotting areas. Maggots inside fruits.
Fruit flies laying eggs in ripening fruits.
Leaf Miner
Serpentine trails or blotches on leaves. Reduced photosynthesis.
Larvae of small flies mining between leaf surfaces.
Thrips Damage
Silver-gray streaks on leaves. Distorted flowers and fruits. Tiny black insects visible.
Thrips feeding on plant tissues and spreading viruses.
Broadleaf Weed Control
Broadleaf weeds competing with crops for nutrients, water, and light.
Various broadleaf weed species invading crop areas.
Grass Weed Control
Grassy weeds competing with crops. Reduced crop yield.
Annual and perennial grass weeds.
Snail and Slug Damage
Irregular holes in leaves. Slime trails visible. Seedlings completely eaten.
Snails and slugs feeding at night on tender plant parts.
Spider Mite Infestation
Fine webbing on leaves. Yellow stippling on leaf surface. Leaf bronzing.
Tiny spider mites sucking plant juices, especially in hot, dry conditions.
Rodent Damage
Gnawed fruits, stems, and roots. Missing seedlings. Burrows near plants.
Rats, mice, and other rodents feeding on crops.
Nitrogen (N) Deficiency
Yellowing of older leaves starting from tips. Stunted growth. Pale green color overall.
Insufficient nitrogen in soil. Leaching in sandy soils.
Phosphorus (P) Deficiency
Dark green or purple leaves. Stunted root development. Delayed flowering.
Low soil phosphorus. Acidic or alkaline soils.
Potassium (K) Deficiency
Yellowing and browning of leaf margins. Weak stems. Poor fruit quality.
Insufficient potassium. Leaching in sandy soils.
Iron (Fe) Deficiency
Interveinal chlorosis on young leaves. Veins remain green.
High soil pH making iron unavailable. Waterlogged soils.
Magnesium (Mg) Deficiency
Interveinal chlorosis on older leaves. Leaf edges curl upward.
Low magnesium. Acidic soils. Excessive potassium.
Calcium (Ca) Deficiency
Blossom end rot in fruits. Tip burn in leaves. Stunted roots.
Low soil calcium. Irregular watering.
Zinc (Zn) Deficiency
Small, narrow leaves (little leaf). Short internodes. Rosetting.
High soil pH. Low organic matter. Excessive phosphorus.
Manganese (Mn) Deficiency
Interveinal chlorosis on young leaves. Tan or brown spots.
High soil pH. Excessive iron or zinc.
